mission.title: ollama: Integrated AMD GPU support
mission.description: Integrated AMD GPU support. Requested by 57+ users.

steps.title: Check current Ollama setup
steps.description:
Verify your Ollama version:
```bash
ollama version
```
This feature addresses AMD integrated GPU (iGPU) support for APUs like AMD 5800U and 7840U.

steps.title: Review AMD iGPU requirements
steps.description:
AMD integrated GPUs use system RAM via GTT/GART for VRAM. Key considerations:
- iGPUs have limited dedicated VRAM (typically 512MB-8GB configurable in BIOS)
- Additional system RAM is available via GTT memory mapping
- ROCm drivers must be installed
- Some iGPUs may need the `HSA_OVERRIDE_GFX_VERSION` environment variable

steps.title: Configure Ollama for AMD iGPU
steps.description:
For Docker with AMD iGPU:
```yaml
services:
  ollama:
    image: ollama/ollama:rocm
    container_name: ollama
    volumes:
      - ollama_data:/root/.ollama
    restart: unless-stopped
    devices:
      - /dev/dri
      - /dev/kfd
    security_opt:
      - "seccomp:unconfined"
    group_add:
      - video
    environment:
      - HSA_OVERRIDE_GFX_VERSION=9.0.0
```
Adjust `HSA_OVERRIDE_GFX_VERSION` for your specific GPU architecture.

steps.title: Verify iGPU acceleration works
steps.description:
Run a model and verify GPU acceleration is active:
```bash
ollama run llama3 "Hello!" --verbose
```
Check the Ollama server logs for GPU detection messages. If the iGPU is not detected, try setting `HSA_OVERRIDE_GFX_VERSION` to match your GPU architecture.

resolution.summary: AMD integrated GPU support was added to Ollama via ROCm. iGPUs use GTT/GART to access system RAM as VRAM. Users may need to set HSA_OVERRIDE_GFX_VERSION and use the ROCm Docker image.

prerequisites.description: A working ollama installation or development environment.
